title: RUN instruction with bind mounted image is never cached
body:
### Issue Description

RUN instruction with bind mounted image is never cached but the same thing using COPY instruction caches ok.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman build the Containerfile bellow
2. build again


```Containerfile
FROM docker.io/library/debian:12

RUN --mount=type=bind,from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:latest,target=/usr/local/bin uv venv /app
```


### Describe the results you received

The RUN instruction is executed both times, it is not taken from cache for the second build.

### Describe the results you expected

The second build should use cache.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

If I change the `Containerfile` (see bellow), everything is cached. The resulting image is the same but the definition is more complex.

```Containerfile
FROM docker.io/library/debian:12 as base

COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:latest /uv /usr/local/bin/uv

RUN uv venv /app

FROM docker.io/library/debian:12

COPY --from=base /app /app
```

comments.body: I _think_ https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/5691 will be adding information about `--mount`ed items to cache evaluation.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

> A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

So?
Looks like the referenced https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/24640#issuecomment-2491659299 is being worked on.  The stale issue is just a but that causes us to relook at older issues.

@mheon yes, that should have fixed this case.  There are related follow-ups in https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/6041 and https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/6110 if you're looking to cherry pick.
I'm going to close this as fixed in the Podman 5.5 release (RC1 is coming out this week), which includes Buildah 1.40 which has the fix
